what_it_does: Central AI generation engine and UI primitives for the Workshop canvas. It defines an immutable diff algebra for deck/brochure/widget/wizard documents, pure apply/invert transforms, Vi prompt builders for block/page/widget/wizard/theme/layout/field generation and whole-doc transforms, a candidate gallery and diff-card UI, a chat/edit bridge, CV_AI capability registration, prompt caching, streaming candidates, heuristic and Vi suggestions, template extraction/materialization, workspace docs and variables stores, and a tethered per-field AI toolbar.
observations: Defines a diff schema covering block, page, widget, wizard, and doc operations plus batching.
Implements reversible edits via invertDiff computed against the document state before application.
All generative moves are registered as CV_AI capabilities so target.kind maps directly to a catalogue entry.
Contains React UI components for candidate galleries, diff cards, transform menus, and a floating field toolbar.
Prompts are JSON-only and include a brand voice loaded from the CV_AI registry at module load.
Streaming generation uses parallel single-shot calls for some targets and a single batched call for others.
Maintains global stores (WS_DOCS, WS_VARS, WS_FIELD, BRIDGE) using window globals for cross-surface coordination.
Includes a 60-second, 200-entry prompt cache and a loose JSON parser to tolerate wrapped LLM output.
Supports workspace variables with {{key}} substitution, reference counting, and AI-driven audit for promotion.
Smart templates strip volatile ids, extract variables via LLM, and materialize new docs with fresh ids.
standouts: Diff algebra with inverse enables undo/redo independent of React state.
CV_AI capability registration makes the catalogue the dispatch layer for every generative action.
Workspace variables and docs store enable live cross-document updates and embeds.
Per-field AI toolbar integrates regeneration and variable promotion directly into inputs.
conventions: Global window.* namespaces for shared modules (WS_AI, WS_BLOCKS, CV_AI, WS_DOCS, WS_VARS, WS_FIELD).
Immutable plain-object diffs and deep cloning via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify).
React hooks aliased with _ai suffix to avoid naming collisions.
Prompts request JSON-only responses and parse them loosely.
Provider resolution always routes through window.CV_AI.resolveProvider('claude').
Loud errors when required registry entries or providers are missing.
concerns: Several functions (classifyIntent, generateEdit, viSuggest, extractTemplate, auditWorkspaceVariables) reference `provider` with `typeof provider !== 'undefined' ? provider : null`, but `provider` is not in scope, so it always resolves to null and falls back to CV_AI.
clone uses JSON serialization, which drops functions, dates, undefined values, and may be slow for large docs.
cachedComplete is defined after the public exports and appears unused by the main completion path.
applyDiff warns on unknown kinds but does not validate diff shape beyond switch cases.
WSFieldToolbar uses the native prompt() dialog and a brittle setTimeout blur check.
materializeTemplate stringifies the doc and runs regex replacement, which can mishandle nested quotes or escaped braces.
notes: This is the Workshop AI engine: a self-contained diff/prompt/UI layer that turns CV_AI capabilities into concrete document edits and renders them as candidate galleries and diff cards.
questions: What is the exact CV_AI registry interface expected by resolveProvider, execute, and get/setActiveSurface?
How and where are WS_BLOCKS renderers registered, and what data shapes do they require?
Which parent components mount WSCandidateGallery, WSDiffCard, WSTransformMenu, and WSFieldToolbar?
Is there a raw Claude endpoint fallback, or is CV_AI always assumed to be present?
How are workspace variables persisted and rehydrated on application startup?
What is the intended relationship between cachedComplete and aiComplete?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose; the file centralizes generation, diff application, UI primitives, and chat bridge, and additionally adds workspace variables, templates, and field-level AI tooling.
apparent_themes: Catalogue-driven dispatch: CV_AI is the single source for capabilities and skills.
Immutable diffs as a universal edit language across document types.
Global bridges for cross-surface coordination and live state sharing.
Human-in-the-loop AI: candidates are generated, previewed, and selectively applied.
Workspace-wide variables and live cross-document consistency.
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a unified, extensible AI editing substrate where every generative action is a registered capability, every edit is a reversible diff, and shared UI primitives surface AI suggestions across deck, brochure, widget, and wizard surfaces.
novelty: The combination of a reversible diff algebra, capability-catalogue dispatch, parallel streaming candidate generation, workspace variable substitution with cross-doc store, and a tethered per-field AI toolbar in a single Workshop engine file.
contribution: A concrete, reusable AI edit substrate—diff schema, apply/invert, candidate generation prompts, gallery and diff UI, and global bridges—that a fused system would keep as the Workshop-side engine for turning AI capabilities into document edits.
summary_for_embedding: AIEngine.jsx is the Workshop AI engine implementing immutable diff transforms, Vi generative capabilities, candidate gallery and diff card UI, chat/edit bridge, and workspace docs/variables stores for ConceptV Studio.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize all AI-driven document editing operations in Workshop behind a single capability catalogue and a reversible diff language.

symbols.does: Deep-clones a value using JSON serialization.
symbols.description: A simple utility that returns null or undefined as-is and otherwise clones via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(o)). It is used to produce immutable copies of documents and sections before applying diffs.

symbols.does: Applies an immutable diff to a document.
symbols.description: Clones the document and executes the operation encoded in the diff, supporting block, page, widget, wizard, doc, and batch kinds. It returns the new document without mutating the original and warns on unknown diff kinds.

symbols.does: Normalizes a section object against its block definition.
symbols.description: Assigns a stable id, merges block defaults from window.WS_BLOCKS with provided data, and preserves kind and locked flags. It is called when building sections from generated or inserted blocks.

symbols.does: Produces a human-readable summary of a diff.
symbols.description: Recursively describes batch diffs and maps individual operation kinds to short English phrases, including block indices, page titles, and the keys being updated.

symbols.does: Returns the human label for a block kind.
symbols.description: Looks up window.WS_BLOCKS[kind].label and falls back to the raw kind string. It is used in candidate labels and diff descriptions.

symbols.does: Loads the ConceptV brand voice text from the CV_AI registry.
symbols.description: An immediately-invoked expression that reads window.CV_AI.get('voice.conceptv') at load time and throws a loud error if the registry or behaviour is missing. It centralizes the voice prompt so it is not duplicated across prompt builders.

symbols.does: Routes a prompt through a resolved AI provider.
symbols.description: Resolves a provider from the argument or the CV_AI registry, optionally caches the result, and calls provider.complete. It enforces that completions go through the registry rather than a raw endpoint.

symbols.does: Extracts a JSON object from a string response.
symbols.description: First tries strict JSON.parse; on failure it searches for the first {...} substring and parses that. It returns null if neither succeeds, tolerating prose wrappers around LLM JSON output.

symbols.does: Lists registered block kinds excluding divider.
symbols.description: A small helper returning Object.keys(window.WS_BLOCKS) filtered to omit the 'divider' kind. It is used to enumerate blocks available for generation.

symbols.does: Builds a compact textual catalog of block data shapes.
symbols.description: Iterates available block kinds and emits a markdown-style list of each kind's label and data field names. The result is included in prompts so Vi knows valid block types.

symbols.does: Summarizes pages surrounding a target page index.
symbols.description: Returns a string with the previous, target, and next pages, listing each page's title and a truncated serialization of its sections. It provides context for generation prompts.

symbols.does: Formats one page's summary line.
symbols.description: Inner helper inside pageContextSummary that serializes a single page's sections into a compact string with kind and truncated data.

symbols.does: Dispatches a generation request to a CV_AI capability.
symbols.description: Validates the document and target, looks up the capability in window.CV_AI by target.kind, and executes it with context, parameters, brief, and surface. It returns a list of candidate objects.

symbols.does: Generates a short random id string.
symbols.description: Returns an 8-character base-36 random string. It is used for candidate ids.

symbols.does: Generates candidate blocks to insert at a page position.
symbols.description: Builds a prompt from brand voice, document title, surrounding pages, available block kinds, and a user hint, asks the provider for JSON candidates, and maps them to block.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Generates alternative versions of an existing block.
symbols.description: Prompts the model with the current block data, other blocks on the same page, and a user hint, then returns block.replace diffs with varied data while preserving kind and id.

symbols.does: Generates candidate pages to insert at a slot.
symbols.description: Summarizes pages before and after the insertion point, asks for full page JSON with title, kind, and sections, and maps results to page.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Generates whole-document transforms from an instruction.
symbols.description: Flattens the top 12 blocks into a prompt, asks for alternative update sets, and returns batch diffs of block.update operations that satisfy the instruction.

symbols.does: Proposes alternative visual themes.
symbols.description: Prompts for theme candidates that are either known theme keys or custom names, paired with rationales, and returns doc.update diffs setting the theme field.

symbols.does: Proposes full page layouts for an empty or selected page.
symbols.description: Uses surrounding context and available block kinds to request complete section lists, then returns a batch diff that clears the page sections and inserts the new ones.

symbols.does: Generates alternative widget data sets.
symbols.description: Prompts with current widget kind, system, and data, asks for different angles while keeping the same shape, and returns widget.data diffs.

symbols.does: Regenerates alternative KPI sets for a widget.
symbols.description: Prompts for count sets of KPIs with different angles, each containing the requested number of {label,value,delta,deltaKind} items, and returns widget.data diffs.

symbols.does: Generates candidate wizard steps to insert.
symbols.description: Summarizes existing steps and insertion position, asks for step JSON, and maps results to wizard.step.insert diffs.

symbols.does: Generates alternative versions of a wizard step.
symbols.description: Prompts with the current step and other step titles, asks for varied patches, and returns wizard.step.update diffs.

symbols.does: Generates alternative values for a single field.
symbols.description: Builds a prompt around current value, block kind, field name, surrounding context, and an angle such as shorter, formal, specific, or different, and returns candidate values without a diff.

symbols.does: Classifies a chat message as edit or question.
symbols.description: Sends the document context and user message to the model with a JSON-only prompt and returns {kind,reason}. It loudly surfaces parse failures rather than defaulting to question.

symbols.does: Generates a batch diff from a free-form edit request.
symbols.description: Builds a prompt describing the document blocks, widget data, or wizard steps and available op kinds, asks for a JSON ops list, and returns a summary plus a batch diff.

symbols.does: Removes ids and timestamps from a document.
symbols.description: Recursively deletes id and createdAt from doc, pages, sections, and steps so template extraction is stable across runs.

symbols.does: Extracts parameterized variables and a templatized document.
symbols.description: Serializes a stripped document, prompts the model to identify 3-7 high-level variables and replace occurrences with {{key}} placeholders, and returns variables plus the templatized doc.

symbols.does: Fills a template with variable values and re-stamps ids.
symbols.description: Stringifies the templatized doc, replaces {{key}} placeholders with provided values, parses it back, and assigns new ids and createdAt to doc, pages, sections, and steps.

symbols.does: Escapes regex metacharacters in a string.
symbols.description: Used when building RegExp objects for placeholder replacement and variable reference counting.

symbols.does: Computes heuristic AI suggestions based on document state.
symbols.description: Inspects the current page, sections, and document type to propose actions such as filling an empty page, adding a CTA or stats page, or applying whole-doc transforms.

symbols.does: React hook that subscribes to the active bridge document.
symbols.description: Returns the current BRIDGE.doc and forces re-render whenever the bridge notifies listeners.

symbols.does: Modal UI for picking among generated candidates.
symbols.description: Renders a grid of candidate cards with previews, keyboard navigation, placeholder skeletons while busy, and a refine input to steer regeneration.

symbols.does: Handles keyboard navigation inside the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: Inner event listener for Escape, arrow keys, Enter, and number keys to close, focus, pick, or directly select candidates.

symbols.does: Renders a preview for a candidate based on its diff.
symbols.description: Applies the candidate diff to the current doc and chooses MiniBlockPreview, MiniPagePreview, MiniWidgetPreview, MiniStepPreview, or a batch stack depending on the diff kind.

symbols.does: Renders a small preview of a block section.
symbols.description: Looks up the block definition and renders a compact, kind-specific preview for headline, hero, body, quote, stats, callout, bullets, metricRow, statPills, and a generic fallback.

symbols.does: Renders a compact page preview.
symbols.description: Shows the page title and small stubs for up to four sections, using block labels from WS_BLOCKS.

symbols.does: Renders a compact widget preview.
symbols.description: Displays eyebrow, title, and up to three KPIs with value and label.

symbols.does: Renders a compact wizard step preview.
symbols.description: Shows step kind, title, body, and up to three fields or options.

symbols.does: Card UI for reviewing and applying a proposed edit.
symbols.description: Displays a summary, a collapsible list of WSDiffLine previews, and Apply/Discard actions.

symbols.does: Renders a single diff line with visual before/after where possible.
symbols.description: For block.update or block.replace it shows side-by-side rendered previews; for block.insert or page.insert it shows the new element; otherwise it falls back to a text description.

symbols.does: Projects CV_AI transform skills into menu items.
symbols.description: Queries the CV_AI registry for layer:skill, family:transform entries and maps them to objects with label, description, instruction, and optional target. It keeps the UI and AI catalogue synchronized.

symbols.does: Dropdown menu of whole-document transforms.
symbols.description: Renders the live projection of transform skills as clickable buttons and calls onPickTransform when one is selected.

symbols.does: Caches and completes a prompt through the Claude provider.
symbols.description: Looks up or stores the result in PROMPT_CACHE, resolves the provider via CV_AI, and calls complete. It is defined after the public exports and appears unused by the main aiComplete path.

symbols.does: Computes the inverse of a diff for undo.
symbols.description: For batch diffs it walks forward to capture intermediate states, then builds reversed operations; for single diffs it returns the complementary operation that restores the prior document state.

symbols.does: Streams candidates as they are generated.
symbols.description: For parallel-friendly targets it fires N independent single-candidate calls and emits each via onCandidate; otherwise it falls back to a single batched generateCandidates call.

symbols.does: Generates a single candidate with a seeded angle.
symbols.description: Appends an angle seed and slot index to the brief and calls generateCandidates with count 1, returning the first candidate.

symbols.does: Reads dismissed suggestion ids from localStorage.
symbols.description: Parses the cvstudio:dismissedSuggestions item into a Set and returns an empty set on parse failure.

symbols.does: Records a suggestion id as dismissed in localStorage.
symbols.description: Adds the id to the stored Set and persists it.

symbols.does: Removes a suggestion id from the dismissed set.
symbols.description: Deletes the id from the stored Set and persists it.

symbols.does: Combines heuristic and Vi-generated suggestions.
symbols.description: Filters heuristics by dismissed state, asks the model for additional concrete actions, and interleaves Vi suggestions ahead of heuristic ones up to a cap.

symbols.does: Renders a real block preview using the block registry.
symbols.description: Looks up the block renderer in window.WS_BLOCKS, wraps it in a scaled container with pointer-events disabled, and passes a no-op setter so contentEditable appears non-mutable.

symbols.does: No-op function used as a placeholder setter.
symbols.description: Inner constant inside RenderedBlockPreview; passed to block renderers to satisfy setter signatures without allowing edits.

symbols.does: Renders a real page preview using block renderers.
symbols.description: Scales a page container, shows the title, and renders up to six sections with their registered block renderers.

symbols.does: React hook subscribing to the global docs store.
symbols.description: Returns the current array of docs from DOCS_STORE and updates when the store notifies.

symbols.does: Replaces {{key}} placeholders in a string with variable values.
symbols.description: Uses the provided vars array or the global VARS_STORE values; it leaves unresolved placeholders intact.

symbols.does: React hook subscribing to the global variables store.
symbols.description: Returns the current variables array and updates when VARS_STORE notifies.

symbols.does: Counts occurrences of each variable across all docs.
symbols.description: Serializes all docs, counts total placeholder matches per variable, and also counts how many distinct docs reference each variable.

symbols.does: Asks Vi to identify values worth promoting to workspace variables.
symbols.description: Walks all docs to collect string values, samples the longest ones, prompts the model to find recurring or high-value candidates, and returns proposed variables.

symbols.does: Recursively collects string values from a document tree.
symbols.description: Inner helper for auditWorkspaceVariables that records every string leaf with its path, skipping id and createdAt keys.

symbols.does: Floating toolbar for per-field AI regeneration and variable promotion.
symbols.description: Follows the focused input element, offers alternate, shorter, formal, and specific rewrites, and can promote the current value to a workspace variable via prompt().

symbols.does: Re-measures the active field's bounding rectangle.
symbols.description: Inner effect callback inside WSFieldToolbar that updates rect on scroll, resize, and layout changes so the toolbar stays anchored.

symbols.does: Triggers a field regeneration with a chosen angle.
symbols.description: Inner handler in WSFieldToolbar that calls the registered regenHandler with the active field info plus angle, then deactivates the toolbar.

symbols.does: Promotes the current field value to a workspace variable.
symbols.description: Inner handler in WSFieldToolbar that suggests a key, creates or updates the variable in WS_VARS, replaces the field value with {{key}}, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Wires a plain input or textarea to the field toolbar.
symbols.description: Adds focus and blur listeners that activate and deactivate WS_FIELD with the element's value, rect, and metadata; it returns a cleanup function.

symbols.does: Activates the field toolbar on focus.
symbols.description: Inner helper inside bindFieldRegen that reads the element's bounding rect and current value and calls window.WS_FIELD.activate.

symbols.does: Deactivates the field toolbar on blur.
symbols.description: Inner helper inside bindFieldRegen that waits briefly and deactivates if focus has not moved into the toolbar itself.
